title: Cancelling a visa - review rights and cancellation process

sections.heading: Cancellation decisions and reasons
sections.section_ref: Home Affairs guidance / Cancelling a visa
sections.text: Department of Home Affairs guidance explains that if a visa is refused or cancelled, the Department will tell the person why the decision was made and whether they have a right to review. For consultation purposes, the cancellation notice or NOICC and any response sent to the Department should be collected first.

sections.heading: Review rights
sections.section_ref: Home Affairs guidance / Review rights after cancellation
sections.text: The guidance says that review rights depend on the kind of decision and that an independent merits review body may be available in some cases. Intake should therefore record who made the decision, when it was made, and whether any review period has already begun to run.

sections.heading: Practical intake focus
sections.text: Because cancellation matters often turn on timing, notice, and response opportunities, a lawyer intake workflow should ask whether the person is in Australia, what notice they received, whether they were invited to comment, and whether any deadline or review period is mentioned in the decision documents.
